title: Tech Disruptors: JFrog CEO on Coding Agents, Supply-Chain Risk

Ben Haim joins Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Mandeep Singh in this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast to discuss the impact of large-language-model coding agents, the challenges of keeping up with model guardrails, and why software supply-chain security and governance are top of mind for modern enterprises. summary: Signs of economic strength alongside easing price pressures spurred a rebound in stocks, with Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh saying inflation risks have come down.

title: This Heatwave Needs to Be Taken Seriously
summary: The heatwave hitting the US right now needs to be taken seriously. Bloomberg Opinion columnist Mark Gongloff has advice on staying cool and how to detect heat exhaustion. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Small Caps Track Largest S&P 500 Lead Since 2003
summary:
The Russell 2000 has outperformed the S&P 500 by almost 14 percentage points during the first six months of this year. Should such a performance gap hold into year-end, it would be the largest in over 20 years.
Jill Carey Hall, Equity Strategist and Head: US SMID Strategy at Bank of America, discusses her mid-year outlook for small cap stocks. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Falls as US-Iran Talks Progress, Supplies Beat Expectations
summary: Oil extended its run of declines as traders monitored US-Iran peace talks, and weekly storage data showed American stockpiles fell by less than anticipated.

title: Warsh Taps Ex-BOE Chief King for New Fed Communications Task Force
summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh has tapped former Bank of England Governor Mervyn King to co-chair the US central bank’s new task force on communications.

title: US Stocks Rise as Traders Weigh Warsh Remarks, Manufacturing
summary: US stocks pared earlier losses as fresh economic data painted a picture of resilience, while traders assessed commentary from Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh.

title: One Almost-Goldilocks Report
summary: While we wait for the jobs data.

title: New York, Chicago Grids Strain as Record Heat Wave Expands
summary:
Power grids from Chicago to New York City are bracing for sky-high demand as sweltering heat begins to build across the eastern half of the US.  
Bloomberg News Meteorologist Rob Carolan breaks down what to expect as temperatures rise. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Bounce on Manufacturing, Warsh’s Remarks
summary:
Signs of economic strength alongside easing price pressures spurred a bounce in stocks from session lows, with Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh saying inflation risks have come down.
Jordan Jackson, Global Market Strategist at JPMorgan Asset Management, gives his macro call and discusses the market reaction he expects to see from Thursday's jobs data. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trans-Oil Seeks to Calm Investors Rattled by Ukraine Report
summary: Trans-Oil Group is organizing calls with bondholders rattled by a report alleging that the founder of the Moldovan grains company breached sanctions on Iran.

title: Nike Appoints Pfizer CFO as New Finance Chief Amid Industry Experience Questions
summary: Nina Trentmann, Senior Editor at Bloomberg News, discussed the recent appointment of Dave Denton, formerly Pfizer's CFO, as Nike's new Finance Chief. While some analysts have expressed concerns about Denton's lack of direct retail and apparel industry experience, Trentmann noted that for large companies like Nike and Pfizer, deep industry knowledge may be less critical than experience managing a complex, global business with a significant market capitalization and extensive supply chains. She speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Eni and Mercuria Agree to Join Forces in Commodity Trading
summary: Italian oil company Eni SpA and commodity merchant Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. signed an agreement to join forces in trading, seeking growth in an area that has seen huge price swings and profit opportunities during the war in Iran.

title: Sugar Touches Highest Since Mid-May as Market Prices in El Niño
summary: Raw sugar futures rose to the highest price in more than a month as fears over El Niño prompt traders to cover short positions.

title: Software Gains as Guggenheim Says AI No Sector ‘Death Knell’
summary: Software shares rose on Wednesday after Guggenheim upgraded a trio of companies, saying fears that artificial intelligence poses a mortal threat to the sector are overdone.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 7/1/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ECB’s Kaasik Says One More Rate Hike Is Reasonable Expectation
summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Ulo Kaasik said further moentary tightening may be needed to ensure inflation returns to target after the Iran war.

title: US Manufacturing Expands at Slower Pace, Costs Gauge Drops
summary: US manufacturing activity expanded for a sixth straight month in June as a war-driven surge in input costs eased.

title: Gold Rises as Traders Weigh Fed Rate Path After Warsh Remarks
summary: Gold rose as traders weighed the outlook for US monetary policy following Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh’s remarks at an annual central banker gathering.

title: Peru’s Inflation Unexpectedly Picks Up With Rising Food Prices
summary: Inflation in Peru’s capital unexpectedly accelerated in June, staying above the central bank’s target range for a fourth straight month as rising food costs add to the recent global energy shock.

title: Pimco Warns Private Credit ‘Confidence Gap’ to Reveal Weak Funds
summary: Private credit investors have become increasingly discerning about dispersion in investment marks, creating a “confidence gap” between asset managers, according to Pacific Investment Management Co.

title: Commodities Broker Pulled Back Into Tax Dividend Fraud Suit by UK Court
summary: ED&F Man Capital Markets Ltd., now MCML Ltd., has been pulled back into a long-running fraud case linked to the Cum-Ex tax dividend scandal after the UK Supreme Court ruled that the Danish tax authority’s lawsuit against the commodities broker can proceed.

title: Alcoa Bets on Aluminum Boom With $5.6 Billion South32 Deal
summary: Alcoa Corp. has agreed to buy South32 Ltd.’s bauxite, alumina and aluminum assets in a deal worth as much as $5.6 billion, cementing its position as a top producer as long-term demand strengthens and the Iran war exposes supply concerns.

title: Alphabet’s $2 Trillion Gain Turns ‘Rock Star’ Into Question Mark
summary: Alphabet Inc. has had a remarkable 12 months by just about any measure you can imagine.

title: Lagarde Says Inflation, Growth Risks Now More Broadly Balanced
summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said risks to euro-area inflation and growth have become less pronounced.

title: Nissan CEO Says It Still Makes Sense to Build Cars in US
summary: Nissan President and CEO Ivan Espinosa says it still makes sense to build cars in the US, despite trade tensions and a weaker yen. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Arabica Gains Most Since 2022 as Supply Risks Rise
summary: Arabica coffee jumped the most in almost four years on Tuesday, buoyed by mounting concerns about supply in top-grower Brazil.

title: US Companies Add 98,000 Jobs in June, ADP Says
summary: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June after a 122,000 increase in the prior month, according to ADP Research data out Wednesday. The median estimate in a Bloomberg survey of economists called for a 120,000 increase. Michael McKee reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Reports at Least $1.4 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings
summary: President Donald Trump reported earning at least $1.4 billion in 2025 from crypto and memecoin-related businesses, according to his latest annual financial disclosure.

title: Fertilizer Firm Draws $12 Billion In Orders in Oman IPO
summary: Oman India Fertiliser Co.’s initial public offering has drawn over $12 billion in orders, in the Middle East’s largest listing since the outbreak of the regional conflict.

title: S&P Winning Streak for July at Risk With Volatile End to Month
summary: July is a great time to buy stocks. In fact, it’s been the best month for the S&P 500 Index in the past two decades. Bulls are finding comfort in that history ahead of what stands to be an eventful stretch.
